description: A potent central-nervous-system stimulant. Rarely prescribed (brand Desoxyn) for ADHD or obesity, it is far better known as the illicit drug 'crystal meth'; its clearance depends strongly on urine pH.

halfLife.conditions: Elimination half-life ~10 h under normal urine pH; the value is strongly URINE-pH DEPENDENT (methamphetamine is a weak base excreted partly unchanged renally): ~6-12 h at neutral/acidic pH, but 16-31 h when urine is alkalinised. Reported similar across routes (IV 11.4 h, intranasal/smoked 10.7 h). Point value 10 h for normal urine pH - the pH dependence is a documented variability caveat (see notes), not a dose-nonlinearity.

vd.conditions: Elimination-phase volume of distribution ~3.73 L/kg from intravenous data (steady-state Vd ~4.2 L/kg); methamphetamine is lipophilic and widely distributed. Treated as a true volume (IV-derived), scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time (~261 L). Low plasma protein binding.

F.conditions: Oral bioavailability is not separately tabulated in the sources; methamphetamine is well and largely completely absorbed orally (INCHEM: absorption 'usually complete by 4 to 6 hours'), and the measured intranasal (79%) and smoked (67-79% of delivered dose) values bracket a high oral F. Modelled as F = 0.67 (a conservative within-bracket estimate); this is the softest oral input and is documented as such.

tmax.conditions: Peak plasma levels 'within 1 to 3 hours' after an oral immediate-release dose (INCHEM PIM 334; ~2 h after a single 12.5 mg dose). Point value 2.5 h; ka derived from this Tmax at derivation time. (Sustained-release oral products push Tmax later and lower - not modelled; the IR case is used.)

F.conditions: Offered as an INFERRED route (available: false) - there is no marketed intravenous methamphetamine product (Desoxyn is oral tablets), but IV is a real route of abuse and the disposition (Vd, half-life) is directly measured in controlled IV studies, so the true-vs-oral contrast can be shown honestly. Labelled 'inferred, not based on route-specific data' by the UI.

variability.notes: Half-life depends strongly on urine pH (renal excretion of unchanged drug is pH-sensitive: faster in acidic urine, much slower when alkaline). Partly metabolised by CYP2D6 (polymorphic) to amphetamine and 4-hydroxymethamphetamine. These modulate exposure but are not used to produce a dose.

cruickshank_2010.title: Cruickshank CC, Dyer KR. A review of the clinical pharmacology of methamphetamine. / Distribution and Pharmacokinetics of Methamphetamine in the Human Body: Clinical Implications (PLoS One 2010, PMC2998419): IV elimination half-life 11.4 h, steady-state Vd 4.2 L/kg (elimination-phase Vd ~3.24-3.73 L/kg), clearance ~0.27 L/h/kg, intranasal bioavailability 79%, smoked 67%; half-life 10-12 h and urine-pH dependent

inchem_pim334.title: INCHEM Poisons Information Monograph 334 (Methamphetamine): oral peak plasma levels within 1-3 h, absorption usually complete by 4-6 h, ~0.02 mcg/mL peak after a single 12.5 mg oral dose at ~2 h; ~30% excreted unchanged in urine at normal pH (up to ~60% acidic, <7% alkaline); biologic half-life 4-5 h orally (16-31 h alkaline urine)

schepers_2003.title: Schepers RJF, Oyler JM, Cone EJ, et al. Methamphetamine and amphetamine pharmacokinetics in oral fluid and plasma after controlled oral methamphetamine administration to human volunteers. Clin Chem. 2003;49(1):121-132 (PMID 12507968): oral S-(+)-methamphetamine, plasma Cmax 14.5-33.8 mcg/L (10 mg) and 26.2-44.3 mcg/L (20 mg), one-compartment model with lag fits the oral data

notes:
d-Methamphetamine - a potent CNS stimulant, one of the most iconic illicit drugs ('crystal meth', Schedule II) and also a marketed oral medicine (Desoxyn, for ADHD/obesity). Modelled as a clean LINEAR one-compartment drug over the studied range; the one-compartment (with lag) model is what Schepers 2003 fit to oral data, and exposure is dose-stable (no saturable elimination at recreational/therapeutic doses).

WHY METHAMPHETAMINE - the long-half-life stimulant and a urine-pH teaching axis. It anchors the classic-illicit-stimulant slot alongside dextroamphetamine (its close structural sibling; methamphetamine is amphetamine plus an N-methyl group and is partly N-demethylated back to amphetamine). Its ~10 h half-life and large lipophilic volume (~3.7 L/kg) give the long duration of action stimulants are known for.

LINEARITY vs the URINE-pH CAVEAT. The dose-response is first-order and superposable (linear: true). The half-life, however, is not a single fixed number: methamphetamine is a weak base excreted partly UNCHANGED by the kidney, and that renal clearance is pH-sensitive - so the half-life runs ~6-12 h at normal/acidic urine pH but stretches to 16-31 h when urine is alkalinised. This is a CONDITIONAL half-life (the lamotrigine-comedication posture): a real dependence on a stated condition, modelled at the normal-urine ~10 h value and documented, NOT auto-applied and NOT a dose-nonlinearity (it does not break superposition of identical doses).

METABOLITE NOTE (why no drawn line). Methamphetamine is partly metabolised to AMPHETAMINE (via CYP2D6 N-demethylation, ~7-15%) - itself an active drug and shipped here as a separate compound (dextroamphetamine). That parent->metabolite pair is NOT drawn because the fraction formed is CYP2D6-genotype-dependent and bimodal (fast vs poor metabolisers), so there is no single citable fm - the same disqualifier that keeps procainamide->NAPA and codeine->morphine out. The relationship is noted here instead.

ROUTES. Oral (Desoxyn, the real marketed route) is available; IV bolus is offered as INFERRED (available: false - no marketed IV product, but IV is a real abuse route and the disposition is directly measured, so the fast-onset IV contrast is shown honestly, the acamprosate/warfarin inferred-IV posture). Smoked and intranasal routes (its common recreational forms) are not modelled - the engine has no inhalational/nasal absorption route. Clearance is deliberately NOT stored (Vd + half-life fix ke; adding CL would be a circular cross-check - the cetirizine rule).

MAGNITUDE CHECK. Built 10 mg oral curve peaks ~21.6 ng/mL near 2.5 h, within the Schepers 10 mg plasma Cmax band (14.5-33.8 mcg/L). EDUCATIONAL ILLUSTRATION ONLY - not for dosing.
